q: The study of the flow of liquid is called ___.
Fluid is a substance that offers no permanent resistance to ___ forces.
The volume of a liquid does not depend on pressure if the ___ is constant.
In the flow of fluids, the path or flow of the fluid particles is called ___.
If fluid particles are moving steady in smooth paths in layers, with each layer moving smoothly past the adjacent layers with no mixing, such a flow is called ___ flow.
In steady laminar flow, streamlines do not ___ each other.
If the flow or path of the fluid particles are irregular, their direction is always changing or whirling, this fluid movement is called ___ flow.
Ideal laminar fluid flow considers that the fluid is ___.
Internal friction in fluids is called ___.
An ideal laminar fluid flow has ___ viscosity.
The equation of continuity states that the liquid entering one end of the pipe leaves from the other end with a constant volume ___.
The larger the area of the pipe, the ___ is the speed.
The smaller the area of the pipe, the ___ is the speed.
Bernoulli's equation relates the pressure, flow speed, and ___ for the steady flow of an ideal incompressible fluid.
Bernoulli's equation can be derived using the ___ principle.
According to work-energy principle, the work done on a section of fluid must equal the change in total ___ energy.
Bernoulli's theorem states that the sum of pressure energy, kinetic energy and potential energy per unit volume of an incompressible, non-viscous fluid in a streamlined laminar flow remains ___.
The air flow moving above the top curved surface of an aeroplane wing has a ___ distance to travel and needs to go faster.
High velocity of air flow causes a ___ pressure above the wing.
When the piston of an insect sprayer is pressed, the fast moving air causes a ___ pressure at the tip of the nozzle.
The ___ effect is a particular manifestation of Bernoulli's theorem that is commonly associated with a spinning object moving through a fluid.
During cyclones, moving air gains speed above the roof of a house causing the ___ pressure on the roof.
Viscosity can be thought of as a measure of a fluid's ___.
A fluid with low viscosity flows ___.
Gases exhibit viscosity, but it is harder to notice in ___ circumstances.
The viscosity of liquids decreases rapidly with an increase in ___.
The viscosity of gases ___ with an increase in temperature.
Viscosity does not change as the amount of matter changes, therefore it is an ___ property.
The SI unit of viscosity is ___ second.
Pascal second is equivalent to newton second per ___.
The SI unit of viscosity is sometimes referred to as the ___.
To measure fluid's resistance to flow when an external force is applied is known as ___ viscosity.
To measure the resistive flow of a fluid under the weight of gravity is known as ___ viscosity.
Newton's law of viscosity states that the shear stress between the two adjacent layers of fluid is directly proportional to the negative value of the ___ gradient.
Shear stress is the shearing force per unit ___.
Viscosity is defined as the ratio of shearing stress to the ___ gradient.
In the equation $\tau = -\eta \frac{dv}{dy}$, the symbol $\eta$ represents the ___ of viscosity.
In a horizontal pipe restriction, if the area decreases, the pressure of water ___.
The unit symbols for pascal second are ___.
The unit $\text{N}\cdot\text{s}\cdot\text{m}^{-2}$ is equivalent to ___.
Fluid statics concentrates on fluids at ___.
Fluid dynamics concerns with the fluid in ___.
Hydrostatic is the study of liquid at ___.
Surface tension and capillarity are important concepts of ___.
A section on surface tension and capillarity is included at the ___ of the chapter.
Motion of fluids is an important application in our daily life, such as ___ flow.
Aviation involves travels in ___.
The density of an incompressible liquid is ___ for any pressure provided that the temperature is kept constant.
In turbulent flow, the speed of the fluid at a point is continuously undergoing changes in both ___ and direction.
The equation of continuity is given by the formula ___.
The resistance to fluid motion (or) internal resistance of fluid is called _.
Shear stress is defined as the _.
Viscosity is defined as the ratio of shearing stress to the _.
Stokes' law expresses the _ force resisting the fall of small spherical objects through a fluid medium.
According to Stokes' law, the drag force acting upward in resistance to the fall of a spherical object in a viscous liquid is given by $F = $ _.
In the upward thrust formula $F_1 = \frac{4}{3} \pi r^3 \rho_2 g$, the symbol $\rho_2$ represents the _.
At a constant velocity of fall called the terminal velocity, the upward and downward forces acting on a sphere are _.
The formula derived for terminal velocity from the balanced forces on a falling sphere yields $\eta = $ _.
The SI unit of surface tension is _.
Surface tension is defined as the force per unit length that acts across any line on a surface tending to _.
Surface tension can also be referred to as a force because it is the tension between _.
The tendency of the surface of a liquid to behave like a stretched elastic skin is called _.
Cohesion refers to forces between molecules of _.
Adhesion refers to forces between molecules of _.
Capillary action is caused by the combination of cohesive forces of the liquid and the _.
a: adhesive forces between the liquid and tube material

q: Capillarity is also the rise or depression of a liquid in a narrow tube of small _.

Liquids that are depressed within thin tubes below the surface of the surrounding liquid _.
An example of a liquid that does not wet glass capillary tubes and is depressed within thin tubes is _.
When a glass tube is dipped into water, the adhesion between glass and water causes a thin film of water to be drawn up over the _.
a: inner and outer surfaces of the tube
